title: SwapVerse
description: SwapVerse was my non-custodial web3 build focused on wallet ownership, transparent transaction flow, and trustworthy on-chain behavior.

content.text: I built SwapVerse after asking myself why users should hand over custody when smart contracts can execute swaps directly.
The product architecture is simple in principle: the smart contract is the execution layer, and the frontend is a clear transaction interface.
I added multi-wallet support and kept approval plus swap as explicit user steps so nothing happened silently.
In web3, clarity is a feature. Users should see and approve every critical step.
Connection state got complicated quickly with account switching, network mismatch, and disconnect events. I replaced simple flags with explicit state transitions and wallet event listeners.
Some friction is not bad UX. Sometimes it is security doing its job.
I also leaned on contract events for transaction history, so the UI reflected on-chain truth instead of guessing from delayed polling.
SwapVerse taught me to treat trust as a measurable product output, not a marketing claim.

content.suggestion: Two-step flow with wallet approval followed by successful token swap confirmation
